meaning: adj.屈服的,被抑制的,柔和的,减弱的
examples: He seemed a bit subdued to me.
The two would sit together on the Heidi's favorite spot, and Peter, quite subdued, behind them.
On the substance, the rhetoric was more cautious -- even subdued for the most part.
Garcia, with a wild swing of emotions whenever he plays, looked subdued on the first tee.
